title: Formatting SQL queries is security-sensitive
description: Formatting SQL queries is security-sensitiveAnalyze your codeintentionality-completemaintainabilitysecuritySecurity Hotspotcwebad-practicesqlFormatted SQL queries can be difficult to maintain, debug and can increase the risk of SQL injection when concatenating untrusted values into the query. However, this rule doesn’t detect SQL injections (unlike ruleS3649), the goal is only to highlight complex/formatted queries.Ask Yourself WhetherSome parts of the query come from untrusted values (like user inputs).The query is repeated/duplicated in other parts of the code.The application must support different types of relational databases.There is a risk if you answered yes to any of those questions.Recommended Secure Coding PracticesUseparameterized queries, prepared statements, or stored procedur
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

logic.detection_conditions: Detects SQL query string formatting (string.Concat, string.Format, string interpolation) before database execution methods (ExecuteSqlCommand, FromSql, SqlCommand, SqlDataAdapter)
logic.triggering_conditions: Triggers when: database execution methods or CommandText assignment contains formatted/concatenated strings
logic.exclusion_conditions: Excludes: parameterized queries (containing @p, @param), logging statements, console output, test code
logic.reasoning: This rule detects security-sensitive SQL query formatting. It targets database execution methods (ExecuteSqlCommand, FromSql, SqlCommand, SqlDataAdapter) and CommandText assignments that receive formatted/concatenated strings, which can lead to SQL injection. Uses regex patterns to identify string formatting operations (Concat, Format, interpolation $"") combined with database operations. Exclusions prevent false positives on parameterized queries (@p0, @param), logging/display code, and test scenarios.

should_trigger: string query = $"SELECT * FROM mytable WHERE mycol={param}"; context.Database.ExecuteSqlCommand(query);
string sensitiveQuery = string.Format("INSERT INTO Users (name) VALUES (\"{0}\")", param); command = new SqlCommand(sensitiveQuery);
